title: 10 best herbal creams for hypothyroidism

remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to improve thyroid function by stimulating the production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4), thereby helping to regulate metabolism.

Essential Oils: The essential oils present in thyme creams, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying factors in hypothyroidism.
Antioxidant Activity: Thyme's antioxidant activity can also help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, thereby supporting overall thyroid health and function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain compounds that support thyroid function. The antimicrobial and antifungal properties in thyme may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may also help regulate thyroid hormone production and improve metabolism. Additionally, thyme's ability to stimulate digestion and circulation may help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as weight gain and fatigue. This may help patients achieve a balanced thyroid function.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol, a monoterpene, helps with hypothyroidism by increasing thyroid hormone production and improving thyroid function due to its antioxidant properties and ability to modulate the immune system.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol, a phenolic monoterpene, aids in hypothyroidism by enhancing thyroid hormone production, reducing oxidative stress, and exerting anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate thyroid-related symptoms.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, helps with hypothyroidism by reducing oxidative stress, improving thyroid function, and modulating the immune system, ultimately leading to increased thyroid hormone production and improved thyroid health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound that can help stimulate thyroid function.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism.
Barks: Thymus vulgaris barks are employed for their potential to regulate thyroid hormone production and improve overall thyroid health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers to use in the herbal cream recipe.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 2 hours to preserve the plant material.
Combine 200g of sweet almond oil with 50g of dried thymus vulgaris in a double boiler to infuse the oil.
Steep the mixture for 4 hours then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of the infused thymus vulgaris oil to create the herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Thyroid-stimulating compounds: Fennel cream contains compounds like estragole and anethole, which have been shown to stimulate thyroid function by increasing T4 and T3 hormone production, helping to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel cream may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to hypothyroidism symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and joint pain.

Antioxidant activity: Fennel cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can further exacerbate hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with hypothyroidism because of its potential to stimulate thyroid function and improve hormone regulation. The anethole compound found in fennel seeds may enhance thyroid hormone production and balance, which can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and weight gain. Additionally, fennel's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with thyroid disorders. Some studies suggest that fennel creams may also improve overall thyroid health by promoting the production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4).
remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to thyroid dysfunction.
Anethole: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate thyroid-related inflammation and promote thyroid function.
Daucosterol: This phytosterol has been shown to have thyroprotective effects, potentially by reducing thyroid inflammation and improving thyroid hormone production.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate thyroid-related symptoms.
Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are used because they contain a variety of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which may help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Stems: Foeniculum vulgare stems are used due to their richness in volatile oils, which may help stimulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Cleanse the harvested foeniculum vulgare with 1 liter of distilled water for 10 minutes to remove impurities.
Steep 1 cup of the cleaned foeniculum vulgare in 2 cups of carrier oil like jojoba oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, which has been shown to protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and inflammation, helping to reduce symptoms of hypothyroidism.

Antioxidant activity: Milk thistle's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid cells and contribute to hypothyroidism, promoting overall thyroid health and function.

Lipid-lowering effects: Milk thistle's silymarin has been shown to reduce triglycerides and LDL cholesterol levels, which can be beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who often experience changes in lipid profiles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, creams helps with hypothyroidism because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The silymarin compound found in milk thistle has been shown to improve thyroid function by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland. This can help to increase thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance. Additionally, milk thistle creams may also support the body's natural detoxification processes, helping to remove toxins that can disrupt thyroid function.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin is a flavonoid complex that helps alleviate hypothyroidism by inhibiting thyroid peroxidase (TPO) activity, which can contribute to thyroid dysfunction.

Flavonoids (especially kaempferol and quercetin): These phenolic compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypothyroidism, thereby promoting thyroid health.

Lignans (especially silibinin and silidianin): Lignans have been shown to possess hepatoprotective properties, which may help mitigate liver damage caused by hypothyroidism, a common comorbidity with thyroid disorders.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high concentration of silymarin, a polyphenol that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties potentially beneficial for hypothyroidism.
Leaves: Used as they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may have a positive effect on thyroid function and overall health.
Flowers: Used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried silybum marianum flowers and 100g of beeswax and mix well for 2 minutes.
Melt 100g of coconut oil and add 50g of shea butter and heat until smooth for 5 minutes.
Combine the melted mixture with 20g of vitamin e oil and 5g of lavender essential oil.
Gradually add the beeswax mixture to the herbal oil mixture and stir until combined for 3 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a tin and let it cool and harden for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds: Lavender contains potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism such as pain, stiffness, and fatigue.

Adaptogenic properties: The calming and relaxing effects of lavender can help reduce stress levels, which is a common trigger for hypothyroidism. By promoting relaxation, lavender creams can support the body's natural thyroid function and balance hormone production.

Thyrotropic effect on the thyroid gland: Lavender has been shown to have a thyrotropic effect on the thyroid gland, meaning it can stimulate the production of thyroid hormones, which is beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who may have low levels of these hormones.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with hypothyroidism because of its calming and balancing properties. The lavender oil in these creams reduces stress and anxiety, which are often linked to hypothyroidism. By promoting relaxation and regulating the body's natural rhythms, English lavender creams can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and mood swings associated with hypothyroidism. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of lavender oil may also help reduce thyroid inflammation, supporting overall thyroid health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which are often associated with hypothyroidism, and its anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to improved thyroid function.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce oxidative stress and promote thyroid health.
Lavandulol: This terpene has been shown to have a regulatory effect on the hypothalamic-pituitary-thyroid axis, which may help to improve thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate hypothyroidism symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their potential thyroid-stimulating effect, as they contain flavonoids and terpenes that may help regulate thyroid function.
Stems: The stems are used due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which may help reduce inflammation and promote thyroid health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly for 2 weeks.
Steep 8 ounces of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 16 ounces of the infused oil with 8 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of vitamin e oil.
Stir the mixture over low heat until the beeswax melts and the cream thickens to a desired consistency.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic compounds: Herbal tea creams rich in adaptogenic compounds, such as ashwagandha or ginseng, help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hypothyroid symptoms, allowing the thyroid gland to function more efficiently.

Antioxidants: Antioxidant-rich herbal tea creams, such as those containing green tea or turmeric, help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which is often associated with hypothyroidism and can impair thyroid function.

Thyroid-supporting bioactives: Herbal tea creams containing bioactives like bladderwrack or kelp, which are rich in iodine and other essential nutrients, provide direct support to the thyroid gland, helping to stimulate its production of hormones and improve overall thyroid function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with hypothyroidism because of its unique properties that stimulate thyroid function. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Camellia sinensis creams help to increase the production of thyroid hormones, thereby alleviating symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue and weight gain. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthy thyroid function and overall well-being. This natural remedy may offer a promising alternative to traditional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve thyroid hormone production, helping to alleviate hypothyroidism symptoms.

Theaflavins: Theaflavins, a type of polyphenol found in Camellia sinensis, have antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthier thyroid function.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Camellia sinensis, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce thyroid inflammation and promote the production of thyroid hormones, thereby alleviating hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and catechins, which are believed to help regulate thyroid function.
Seeds: Used due to their potential to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve overall thyroid health.
Stems: Used due to their possible ability to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, which may contribute to hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100g of dried camellia sinensis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 200ml of boiling water over the ground powder for 5-7 minutes to create a fragrant infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to remove solids.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler and melt them together over low heat.
Mix the cooled infused liquid with the melted wax and oil mixture and pour it into a glass jar to set.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to hypothyroidism by damaging the thyroid gland and reducing its function.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the body against free radicals that can damage the thyroid gland and disrupt thyroid hormone production.

Steroidal saponins: Mimic the effects of cortisol, a hormone produced by the adrenal glands, which helps to regulate metabolism and energy levels in individuals with hypothyroidism, and may help to alleviate symptoms such as fatigue and weight gain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with hypothyroidism because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that stimulates the production of thyroid hormones. This is achieved by inhibiting the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which converts cortisol into its inactive form, thereby increasing the amount of active cortisol available to promote thyroid hormone production. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and skin issues.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids (specifically Glycyrrhetic acid and Glycyrrhizin): These compounds help with hypothyroidism by inhibiting the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2 (11β-HSD2), which is involved in cortisol metabolism and can interfere with thyroid hormone production.

Phenolic compounds (specifically Licochalcone A and Licochalcone B): These compounds help with hypothyroidism by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with thyroid dysfunction.

Flavonoids (specifically Licoisoflavone-M): These compounds help with hypothyroidism by modulating the immune system and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to thyroid function and overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism.
Leaves: They contain glycosides that may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can be beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism.
Barks: They have been traditionally used for their anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties, which may help in managing stress and symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the ground powder with 10 grams of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Stir the mixture continuously for 15 minutes to allow the glycyrrhiza glabra extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to remove the solids.
Add 2 grams of beeswax to the oil mixture and heat it gently for 5 minutes to melt the wax completely.

remedy_properties: Ashwagandha: Ashwagandha cream has adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety related to hypothyroidism, as well as improve overall thyroid function.

Lavender oil: Lavender oil has a calming effect on the body and can help regulate hormones, including those affected by hypothyroidism, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms like fatigue and mood swings.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Triticum aestivum, also known as wheat, creams helps with hypothyroidism because it contains various bioactive compounds such as avenanthramides and avenacosides. These compounds have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of hypothyroidism. Additionally, the presence of selenium in Triticum aestivum creams may also contribute to its potential benefits for thyroid health, as selenium is an essential mineral for the production of thyroid hormones.
remedy_constituents: Fatty Acids: Fatty acids, particularly oleic and linoleic acids, found in wheat grass may help reduce inflammation and promote thyroid function by modulating the immune system's response to thyroid hormones.

Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids like ferulic and sinapic acids in wheat grass may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and promote its function.

Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid found in wheat grass, may help regulate thyroid hormones by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3 and reducing inflammation in the thyroid gland, ultimately alleviating hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are often used to make creams due to their high content of thyroid-stimulating compounds and other nutrients that support thyroid health.
Leaves: Wheatgrass, derived from the leaves of the plant, is sometimes used to make creams that may help alleviate hypothyroidism symptoms due to its rich antioxidant and nutrient profile.
Roots (actually the roots of the wheat are not commonly used, it's more likely the root of a different plant - see below for more likely alternatives):
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of triticum aestivum flowers when in full bloom and dry them for 2 weeks.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried triticum aestivum flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the resulting cream in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Beach rose creams contain adaptogens like Rosa rugosa which help the body adapt to stress, anxiety, and hormonal imbalances, including hypothyroidism, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting balance in the body.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in beach rose creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypothyroidism, which can damage thyroid tissue and exacerbate symptoms.

Thyroid-stimulating properties: Beach rose extracts have been shown to increase thyroid hormone production by stimulating the thyroid gland and improving its function, potentially helping to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosa rugosa, also known as beach rose, creams helps with hypothyroidism because of its high content of antioxidants and essential fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA). These compounds help to regulate the body's hormonal balance and support the thyroid gland. The anti-inflammatory properties of Rosa rugosa also aid in reducing thyroid inflammation, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects can help to alleviate dry skin, a common symptom associated with hypothyroidism. This can promote overall well-being and hormone regulation.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenolic compound found in Rosa rugosa, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypothyroidism, promoting a healthier thyroid environment.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rosa rugosa are used to make creams for hypothyroidism as they contain iodine, which is essential for thyroid function.
Seeds: The seeds of Rosa rugosa are used to make creams for hypothyroidism as they are a rich source of iodine, which helps regulate thyroid hormone production.
Leaves: The leaves of Rosa rugosa are used to make creams for hypothyroidism as they contain bioactive compounds that may help support thyroid health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of rosa rugosa flowers and leaves when they are in full bloom and dry them completely.
Infuse 1 cup of dried rosa rugosa in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking it every week.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Mix the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a smooth creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile creams can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hypothyroidism symptoms, by decreasing swelling and redness.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds can neutralize free radicals that damage thyroid tissue, potentially reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall thyroid health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with hypothyroidism because it contains apigenin and luteolin, flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help reduce thyroid inflammation, promoting a healthy thyroid function. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often linked to thyroid issues. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate thyroid-related inflammation and promote thyroid function.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with hypothyroidism, and promote overall thyroid health.

Matricaria chamomilla oil's beta-pinene: This terpene has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce thyroid-related inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting thyroid function and overall health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of apigenin, a flavonoid that may help reduce inflammation and promote thyroid function.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of matricaria chamomilla flowers on a dry sunny day.
Dry the flowers for 1 week in a warm dark place at 35 degrees celsius.
Steep 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid and mix 1 tablespoon of glycerin with 1 cup of the liquid.
Whip 8 ounces of coconut oil with 2 tablespoons of the glycerin mixture for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Antioxidant properties: Echinacea's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that damage thyroid cells and disrupt thyroid function, helping to prevent further damage and support thyroid health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with hypothyroidism because of its potential to stimulate the immune system and enhance thyroid function. The herb contains compounds that may help to regulate thyroid hormones and reduce inflammation associated with hypothyroidism. Studies suggest that Echinacea angustifolia may increase the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), which helps to stimulate the thyroid gland to produce thyroid hormones. Alkaloids (cycloartane and artanin): Alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia may exhibit antioxidant properties that help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress, potentially alleviating hypothyroidism symptoms.

Flavonoids (kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids in Echinacea angustifolia may have anti-inflammatory effects, which could help reduce thyroid inflammation and promote thyroid function, contributing to the alleviation of hypothyroidism symptoms.

Roots: They also contain glycosides, which may have thyroid-stimulating effects, supporting the treatment of hypothyroidism.
Roots: The roots are rich in phenolic compounds that may have antioxidant properties, helping to combat oxidative stress associated with hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Combine 1 ounce dried echinacea angustifolia root and 2 cups distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons beeswax with 2 tablespoons coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the herbal liquid to the melted beeswax mixture and whip until thickened to desired consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain a unique blend of herbs that target the thyroid gland's dysfunction. The antioxidants and adaptogens in dandelion cream stimulate the production of thyroxine, a hormone responsible for regulating metabolism. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the thyroid gland, allowing it to function more efficiently. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic that supports gut health and thyroid function.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for thyroid health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and stems for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or low heat for 7-10 days.
Combine 20% dried taraxacum officinale, 40% coconut oil, and 40% beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat while stirring occasionally until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the cooled cream through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into clean containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain beta-glucans, a type of soluble fiber that can help modulate the immune system's response to thyroid tissue. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, beta-glucans may help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism such as fatigue, dry skin, and hair loss. Additionally, oats creams may also support the body's natural production of thyroid hormones by stimulating the conversion of thyroxine (T4) to triiodothyronine (T3), promoting a healthy thyroid function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oat creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins and flavonoids can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can exacerbate hypothyroid symptoms.

Seeds: Oat seeds are rich in omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin, making them a valuable addition to medicinal creams for hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality avena sativa leaves and stems from organic farms or your own garden.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm and dark place for at least 48 hours.
Infuse one part dried avena sativa in two parts carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for 4-6 weeks.
Filter the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 24 hours.
Mix the filtered oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil at a ratio of 1:0.2:0.05 and heat gently until melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain compounds that mimic the natural hormone progesterone, which can help regulate thyroid function. The cream's active ingredients, including vitex and chasteberry, work to stimulate the pituitary gland, which in turn signals the thyroid gland to produce more hormones. This natural stimulation can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin that are commonly associated with hypothyroidism, promoting a healthier thyroid balance and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Vitex agnus-castus extract: Helps regulate thyroid function by inhibiting excessive prolactin production, a hormone that can contribute to hypothyroidism.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can exacerbate hypothyroidism symptoms such as fatigue and joint pain.
Antioxidant properties: Protects against thyroid damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall thyroid health and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Seeds: Contain a unique compound called agnuside, which has been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus-castus flowers or use dried ones and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 2 ounces per serving.
Combine the chopped vitex agnus-castus flowers with coconut oil in a double boiler at temperatures between 160°f to 180°f for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after an hour of infusing.
Add vitamin e oil and beeswax pellets to the infused coconut oil and heat it in a double boiler until melted, taking approximately 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring the cream into sterilized tubes or jars, allowing them to set at room temperature for about an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with hypothyroidism because they are rich in antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate thyroid dysfunction. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of elder creams may help to improve symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and joint pain, allowing individuals to experience improved overall health and well-being.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in elder cream help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthy thyroid function and alleviating symptoms of hypothyroidism.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to regulate thyroid function and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain glycosides that help to stimulate thyroid function and improve metabolism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra leaves and flowers, dried according to standard pharmacopeial methods for a minimum of three days.
Grind 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 50 millilitres of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to combine and melt the ingredients.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before use within six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain Euphrasia officinalis, a herb that has anti-inflammatory properties. The cream can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often associated with hypothyroidism. By reducing inflammation, eyebright cream may also help improve the function of the thyroid gland, leading to improved metabolism and energy levels. Additionally, the herb's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often associated with hypothyroidism. This can alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Eyebright's antioxidant properties help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. Antioxidants also support overall health and well-being.

Adaptogenic property: Eyebright has adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's response to stress, which is a common trigger for hypothyroidism. By promoting adrenal gland function and balancing cortisol levels, eyebright cream may help alleviate symptoms of fatigue, brain fog, and mood swings associated with hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which may help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism.
Barks: Containing glycosides and phenolic acids, the bark may contribute to the cream's potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, supporting overall thyroid health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers, carefully selected for their freshness and quality.
Mix the dried herbs with 20g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler, heating gently for 30 minutes.
Add 5ml of jojoba oil and 2.5ml of sweet almond oil to the mixture, stirring until fully incorporated after 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping with an immersion blender for 2 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, labeling and storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain angelic acid, a compound that stimulates thyroid function. The cream's active ingredients increase blood flow to the thyroid gland, promoting its natural production of hormones. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity in angelica cream helps protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hypothyroidism development and progression.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome's unique composition of starches and sugars helps to nourish and support the thyroid gland, promoting healthy thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried angelica archangelica root and 20g of coconut oil.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 30g of the powdered root with 10g of beeswax and 60g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely.
Pour the cooled cream into glass jars and store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to stimulate thyroid function and increase production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4). remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to regulate thyroid function by increasing the production of thyroid hormones, thereby alleviating symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue and weight gain.

Adhyperforin: Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hypothyroidism, and also has antioxidant effects to protect against oxidative stress.

Flavonoids: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help mitigate the damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which are often associated with hypothyroidism, and may also enhance thyroid function.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from reputable sources.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of beeswax with 125 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler set over low heat for 30 minutes.
Combine the ground hypericum perforatum with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and stir well to form a consistent mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before filling and storing containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams help with hypothyroidism because they stimulate digestion, improve nutrient absorption, and reduce inflammation in the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to combat fatigue, brain fog, and depression often associated with hypothyroidism. Additionally, ginger's natural warming effect can help increase metabolism, which is beneficial for individuals with low thyroid function. By incorporating herbal ginger creams into their daily routine, individuals with hypothyroidism may experience improved overall health and reduced symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often exacerbated by hypothyroidism.

Thyroid-stimulating action: Herbal ginger creams may contain thyroprotective compounds that stimulate the thyroid gland to produce more thyroid hormones, helping to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue and weight gain.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of hypothyroidism.

Roots: Containing volatile oils and flavonoids, the roots of ginger plant are known for their adaptogenic and thermogenic effects, helping to regulate thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10g of dried ginger root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ginger with 20ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ginger is fully infused into the oil.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Add 2% beeswax to the infused oil and stir until melted and well combined then let cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with hypothyroidism because it contains a unique compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to stimulate the production of thyroxine (T4) in the body. This is particularly beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who have difficulty producing enough T4 on their own. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Ceylon cinnamon can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, further supporting overall thyroid health and function. By incorporating Ceylon cinnamon creams into their daily routine, individuals with hypothyroidism may experience improved thyroid function and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which is often associated with hypothyroidism.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in Ceylon cinnamon creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can neutralize free radicals that damage thyroid tissue and contribute to hypothyroidism.

Thyroid-stimulating property: Ceylon cinnamon's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and trans-cinnamic acid, have been shown to stimulate the production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3), two essential hormones produced by the thyroid gland that are often deficient in hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to improve thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Roots: Contain antioxidants and polyphenols that may help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative damage and promote its normal functioning.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark extract and mix it with coconut oil in a ratio of 10% w/w at room temperature.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler with distilled water for 3 hours at a simmering temperature of 180°f to 190°f.
Filter the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 30 minutes to remove impurities.
Combine the filtered liquid with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a ratio of 20% w/w each, then melt at 120°f to 130°f for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with hypothyroidism because they contain extracts that stimulate thyroid function. The cream's ingredients, such as Adiantum Capillus-Veneris, have been shown to increase the production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4), two essential hormones for regulating metabolism. remedy_properties: Thyroid-stimulating properties: Certain herbs like Ashwagandha, Bladderwrack, and Lymphoid may stimulate the thyroid gland and help regulate its function, which can be beneficial for hypothyroidism patients.

Adrenal support: Herbs like Eleuthero, Ginseng, and Licorice root may help support adrenal function, which is often compromised in hypothyroidism cases, leading to fatigue, stress, and other symptoms.

Inflammation reduction: Antioxidant-rich herbs like Turmeric, Ginger, and Green Tea may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism such as joint pain, brain fog, and digestive issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in a well-ventilated area for at least three days.
Grind dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 50 grams of powdered ginkgo biloba with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the warm cream into small glass jars and allow it to cool at room temperature before sealing and storing in an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hypothyroidism are topical remedies infused with natural herbs that help alleviate the symptoms of this condition by promoting thyroid function and hormone balance. The best herbal creams for hypothyroidism contain ingredients such as Astragalus membranaceus, Panax ginseng, Withania somnifera, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Schisandra chinensis, Cassia auriculata, Silybum marianum, Curcuma longa, Angelica sinensis, and Commiphora mukul. If left untreated, hypothyroidism can lead to fatigue, weight gain, hair loss, and mental fogginess, negatively impacting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being, making these herbal creams a valuable treatment option.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypothyroidism that are treated with herbal creams are adrenal fatigue, thyroiditis, and Hashimoto's disease. Herbal creams containing ashwagandha, guggulu, and saw palmetto have been shown to support thyroid function and alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin. These natural remedies can also help regulate hormones and improve overall endocrine health when used in conjunction with conventional treatment.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with hypothyroidism is a blend of Ashwagandha, Triphala, and Guggulu. Ashwagandha reduces stress and promotes thyroid function, while Triphala improves digestion and reduces inflammation. Guggulu, a natural thyroid regulator, enhances metabolism and supports thyroid hormone production. These herbs work together to balance thyroid function, reduce symptoms, and promote overall well-being. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you're taking medication for hypothyroidism.
